checks.finding: All 13 tasks' Risk tiers match their scope per risk-classification-policy.md. Sensitive-surface tasks are high: T-002 (github-client SSRF/error-normalization), T-003 (auth/token scrubbing/secrets), T-004 (repo-resolve SSRF/input guard), T-005/T-012/T-013 (public API contract = sentinel surface), T-006 (read/write-separation static gate; access control), T-007 (public contract schema), T-009/T-010 (installer data mutation/deletion of user config). Non-sensitive tasks are correctly lower: T-001 (scaffold/envelope, sensitive parts deferred to blocked successors) medium, T-008 (dist/CI/smoke) medium, T-011 (docs/traceability) low. No under-classification of a sentinel surface. T-006's scope is test/static files only, but it is not over-classification: security-spec.md STRIDE/OWASP (TEST-006/TEST-007, Broken Access Control) designates it the mechanical chokepoint enforcing REQ-003 read/write separation, so tdd Red->Green (write injected -> check fails; clean src -> passes) is meaningful assurance, not wasted effort.

No issues found. Every high task (T-002,T-003,T-004,T-005,T-012,T-013,T-006,T-007,T-009,T-010) includes both mandatory Done When items: 'Red->Green evidence 記録(tdd)' and '独立レビュー verdict PASS が evidence に記録される' (plus provenance/spec_revision evidence bundle). No task is Risk: critical, so second-approver/signed-bundle items are not required.

No issues found. All tasks have <=8 Done When items and <=3 distinct implementation areas; no multi-phase titles. The attempt-2 r2 split (oversized T-005 -> T-005 run-tools / T-012 jobs+artifacts / T-013 get_job_log+256KiB truncation) yields three right-sized, coherent tool-implementation units with genuine integration concerns (server.ts registration, github-client/auth/repo-resolve wiring, ring-buffer truncation), not fragmentation. No rename-only or single-function-edit fragmented tasks.

No issues found. Every functional task maps to an error-path/edge-case AC: T-002->AC-010 (401/403/404/429/5xx/network all branches, no upstream-body relay) + AC-011; T-003->AC-008 (token-unset auth-missing) + AC-009 (canary no-leak); T-004->AC-012 (unresolvable owner/repo -> invalid-input); T-005->AC-002 (nonexistent run id -> not-found); T-012->AC-005 (expired artifact edge case); T-013->AC-004 (>256 KiB tail-priority truncation). T-001 (scaffold) error-envelope branch is exercised by downstream error-path ACs (AC-010/AC-013).

No issues found. Declared test types match scope: AC-001..005/008..011/016..018 integration (tool+fake GitHub API or installer+config = >=2 components); AC-007 static (grep) src-wide scan; AC-006/AC-012 unit+static (single-module/input-schema boundary + static exec check); AC-013 unit (schema/ajv single contract boundary); AC-014 CI (dist-parity); AC-015 smoke (stdio startup + tools/list enumeration); AC-019 doc review. No e2e/acceptance mislabeling.

No issues found. Every high task carries an explicit Rollback: field. Config-mutating tasks add design-level safety beyond commit revert: T-009 notes broken-JSON fail-safe preventing irreversible config damage; T-010 notes 'only installer-managed ci-mcp entry deleted' misdelete guard. Revert-commit rollback is appropriate for this pre-release, unshipped MCP; successors are gated by Blockers so reverting has no downstream impact.

No issues found. The r3 fix holds: T-005/T-012/T-013 share src/tools/actions.ts and src/server.ts but run under a strict serial chain (T-005 <- T-012 <- T-013 via Blockers), so no uncoordinated parallel overlap. Other shared files are create-then-extend with transitive ordering: package.json (T-001 creates, T-008 adds build script; T-008 transitively after T-001), tests/install.tests.ps1 (T-009 install cases, T-010 uninstall cases; T-010 <- T-009). All other src modules are owned by a single task.

No issues found. Blockers match logical artifact dependencies: T-002<-T-001 (envelope), T-003<-T-002 (auth wires token into github-client), T-004<-T-001 only (repo-resolve independent of client/auth; attempt-1 false dependency correctly removed), T-005/T-012/T-013 depend on github-client+auth+repo-resolve, T-006/T-007 depend on all 5 tools, T-008<-T-006/T-007, T-009<-T-008 (needs built dist), T-010<-T-009, T-011<-all. Explicit T-002/T-003/T-004 blockers on T-012/T-013 are transitively-true (not spurious) and do not inflate the critical path. No missing blockers that would cause integration failure.

SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope. All 13 tasks are new-implementation, distribution, installer, or documentation work; none is scoped as a bugfix, regression fix, debugging, flaky-test, or incident-remediation task.
